sections.title: Introduction to Plus-que-parfait
sections.content: The plus-que-parfait (also called the past perfect) is used to describe an action that had already been completed before another past action. It is the past of the past. In English, it corresponds to 'had + past participle'.
examples: J'avais déjà mangé quand il est arrivé (I had already eaten when he arrived)
Elle était partie avant que je ne revienne (She had left before I returned)
Nous avions fini nos devoirs quand le film a commencé (We had finished our homework when the movie started)

sections.title: Formation
sections.content: The plus-que-parfait is formed with the imparfait of the auxiliary verb (avoir or être) + the past participle of the main verb. The same rules apply as for passé composé regarding which auxiliary to use.
examples: J'avais mangé (I had eaten) - auxiliary avoir
Tu avais fini (You had finished) - auxiliary avoir
Il était parti (He had left) - auxiliary être
Nous étions arrivés (We had arrived) - auxiliary être

sections.title: With Avoir
sections.content: Most verbs use avoir as the auxiliary in the plus-que-parfait, just like in the passé composé.
examples: J'avais parlé à mon ami (I had spoken to my friend)
Tu avais écrit une lettre (You had written a letter)
Il avait vu ce film (He had seen that movie)
Nous avions compris la leçon (We had understood the lesson)

sections.title: With Être
sections.content: Verbs that use être in the passé composé also use être in the plus-que-parfait. Remember that past participle agreement rules still apply.
examples: J'étais allé(e) au musée (I had gone to the museum)
Tu étais venu(e) me voir (You had come to see me)
Il était monté dans sa chambre (He had gone up to his room)
Nous étions partis en vacances (We had left on vacation)

sections.title: Sequence of Past Tenses
sections.content: In a sentence with two past actions, use the plus-que-parfait for the action that happened first (the more distant past) and the passé composé for the action that happened second.
examples: Quand je suis arrivé, le film avait déjà commencé (When I arrived, the movie had already started)
Il a mangé le gâteau qu'elle avait préparé (He ate the cake that she had prepared)
Nous avons rencontré l'homme qui était venu la veille (We met the man who had come the day before)

sections.title: Negation
sections.content: Negate the plus-que-parfait by placing ne/n' before the auxiliary and pas after: ne + imparfait auxiliary + pas + past participle.
examples: Je n'avais pas mangé (I had not eaten)
Il n'était pas parti (He had not left)
Nous n'avions pas fini (We had not finished)

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Write 5 sentences using the plus-que-parfait. Each sentence should have two past actions where one happened before the other.
